name: Herbfarm Rosemary Shortbread
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup White or brown rice flour
recipeInstructions:
1. Line two baking sheets with parchment paper.
2. Cream butter in a large bowl until pale yellow and light.
3. Add the sugar; continue mixing until the mixture is fluffy.
4. If using fresh rosemary, mince it. If using dried rosemary, crush it in a mortar and pestle or by using a rolling pin on a flat surface until it is fine but not a powder.
5. Add both flours, the salt, and the rosemary to the butter mixture; mix until thoroughly combined. The dough will be somewhat soft.
6. Refrigerate it for 1 hour, if necessary, so that it is firm enough to roll.
7. Roll the dough out to form a rectangle about 10 x 14 inches and 1/4 inch thick.
8. Cut cookies into 1 1/2 x 2 inches rectangles or any other shape you like.
9. Place them about 1/2 inch apart on the prepared baking sheets.
10. Sprinkle with sugar topping, if desired.
11. Bake at 375°F in the center of the oven until they are golden at the edges, 12 to 15 minutes.
12. Remove from the oven and transfer to wire racks to cool.
13. Pack in an airtight container.
14. The cookies will keep, their flavor improving, for at least a week.
